title: A7 JSON-edit-mode toggle — hidden keyboard shortcut surfaces raw JSON textarea + save (escape hatch for power users while A1 dogfoods)
close_reason: A7 verified: 53 editor specs (47+6), 320 workspace, build green. JsonEditMode behind Cmd+Shift+J / Ctrl+Shift+J. validateDoc + parse-error gates block save. Esc closes with unsaved-confirm. Commit afd0d8f.

title:
A1b inline-rich-text on action label — TipTap inline-only config, no

wrapping (~40 LOC)
close_reason: A1b verified: 47 editor specs (41+6), 314 workspace, build green. RichTextLabel uses inline-only TipTap config (Document + Text, no paragraph wrapper); editor.getText() strips marks on save so AST stays string. ActionBlock.label unchanged. A1 chain complete: every text input in the editor is now TipTap-driven. Commit 1db6890.

title: A1a inline-rich-text on paragraph + callout body + list item — TipTap shared config (~110 LOC)
close_reason: A1a verified: RichTextField wires TipTap into paragraph/callout-body/list-item; action label stays on input for A1b. 41 editor specs (33+8), 308 workspace, build green. inline-node-tiptap.ts refactored cleanly out of __experiments__/ with full type coverage. jsdom env override per grill q10. A4/A5/C1 unblocked. Commit 280c956.

title: dev-workflow-doc — pnpm dev:full + README updates
description: dev-workflow-doc — pnpm dev:full script (concurrently runs Vite + MCP HTTP server) + README updates. Document VITE_PORTABLE_DOC_MCP_URL override. Document the retry-banner workflow from C1. ~10 LOC + a few README sections. Depends on C1.

title: editor-via-mcp-http — replace direct backend imports with StreamableHTTPClientTransport
description: editor-via-mcp-http — replace direct backend imports with StreamableHTTPClientTransport. Per grill q8: BANNER + RETRY-ON-CLICK button + fall back to direct imports if retry fails. Banner copy: 'MCP server not running — start with pnpm dev:full, then click retry. Editor running on direct backends until then.' Keeps dogfood story visible while degrading gracefully. ~70 LOC + ~40 tests. Depends on A1a.

title: playground-copy-and-share — copy buttons + ?doc= base64-gzip URL state
description: playground-copy-and-share — copy buttons per surface + ?doc=base64-gzip URL state. URL length warning at 2000 chars (per grill q7: email-paste-safe threshold; Outlook/Apple Mail truncate around there). Visitors exceeding 2000 still see warning but URL works on Twitter/Discord/Slack. ~60 LOC + ~40 tests. Depends on B1.

title: playground-5-surface-render — Web/Email/TUI/Native/Text tabs (kernel-direct)
description: playground-5-surface-render — Web/Email/TUI/Native/Text tabs (kernel-direct). HAND-ROLLED truecolor-aware ANSI parser (~80 LOC) for browser TUI rendering — NO ansi_up dep (per grill q6: renderInk emits exactly what we control; saves 10 KB on the public playground; tests double as documentation of what renderInk produces). Tab order: Web → Email → TUI → Native → Text. ~180 LOC + ~100 tests. Depends on B1.

title: playground-paste-and-validate — JSON textarea + live validateDoc + fixture loaders
close_reason: B1 verified: paste-box defaulting to welcome fixture, Welcome/Incident loaders, 300ms debounced live validateDoc surfacing parse errors + rule-tagged issues. 4 new playground specs, workspace 330 specs green, both builds clean. Commit ccdefb0.

title: live-preview-strip — 5 small thumbnails in editor header
description: live-preview-strip — 5 thumbnails in header REPLACE the current 5 tabs (per grill q12). Thumbnails ARE the surface switcher — labels under each (Web/Email/TUI/Native/Text). Click switches the right-panel preview. Thumbnails update on doc edit (debounced 500ms). One UI element doing the job of two — cleaner. ~80 LOC + ~50 tests.
close_reason: A6 verified: PreviewStrip replaces PreviewTabs (deleted), 5 lazy-mounted thumbnails with 500ms debounce, lazy Web chunk preserved (94 KB). 59 editor specs (53+6), 326 workspace, build green. Commit 9353554.

title: inline-diagnostics — red dots on tiles for validateDoc issues
description: inline-diagnostics — block-level red dots on tiles for validateDoc issues. Per grill q5: block-level is right-sized for v0.3 (the 10 ValidationIssue types are mostly prop-level). Char-range decoration is v0.4 territory. Click red dot → scroll to offending block + highlight variant picker tile that fixes it. ~70 LOC + ~50 tests. Depends on A1a.

title: visual-variant-picker — thumbnail grid replacing dropdowns
description: visual-variant-picker — thumbnail grid replacing tone+emphasis dropdowns. HYBRID rendering per grill q4: direct PdStyle→CSS for callout variants (tone+border-only, visually identical to backend); backend-web/static round-trip for action.primary + action.secondary variants (button shape + VML differs materially). Hybrid keeps picker honest where it matters and fast where it doesn't. ~140 LOC source + ~80 tests. Depends on A1a.

title: slash-command-insertion — popover with type filter + keyboard nav
description: slash-command-insertion — popover with type filter, keyboard nav. Filter algorithm: substring first, then HAND-ROLLED LEVENSHTEIN (~30 LOC, distance≤2 fallback after substring miss) for typo tolerance. NO fuse.js (~6 KB not worth it for 10 candidates). Per grill q3.

title: block-shaped-tiles — left panel tiles with type icons + tone hints
description: block-shaped-tiles + drag-and-drop reorder — left-panel tiles with type icons, tone color hints (callout/action), DRAG-AND-DROP reorder via dnd-kit (~10 KB dep + ~80 LOC integration). Up/down buttons stay as keyboard fallback. ~200 LOC source + ~100 tests. Per grill q2: a non-functional drag handle is a UX lie; either ship DnD or drop the handle. v0.3 ships DnD.
close_reason: A2 verified: BlockTile + BlockList with dnd-kit Pointer+KeyboardSensor, tone stripe for callout, type icons + content preview + drag handle. 85 editor specs (59+26), 356 workspace, build clean. Commit 3ae9ccb.

title: inline-rich-text-on-blocks — TipTap on 4 text-bearing contexts (paragraph, callout body, list item, action label)
close_reason:
Split per grill q1 into A1a (paragraph + callout body + list item, ~110 LOC, shared TipTap config) + A1b (action label, ~40 LOC, inline-only, no

wrapping). Configs differ enough that bundling clouds review and forces both to land before A4/A5/C1 unblock. The 2-way split matches the natural code seam.

title: prototype-mcp-http — wire --http flag + curl-verifiable doc_validate
close_reason: T5 verified: 36 mcp-server specs (24+12), 300 workspace, --http/--port/--cors-origin flags wired, bind 127.0.0.1 only, stdio branch preserved, zero new deps. Curl confirms tools/list + tools/call doc_validate roundtrip + CORS 403 enforcement. Production-ready code carries forward into v0.3 build phase. Commit 2dcc67d.

title: scaffold-playground-app — apps/playground/ + GH Pages deploy
description: scaffold-playground-app — apps/playground/ with Vite static build. Imports validateDoc DIRECTLY (no MCP integration; playground bypasses MCP per locked decision). Deploys at site root (frikkern.github.io/portable-doc/). CI workflow uses actions/cache@v4 for pnpm + retry on deploy step. Renders 'PortableDoc Playground — coming soon' placeholder.
close_reason: T3 verified: apps/playground/ scaffolded with Vite base /portable-doc/, kernel-direct validateDoc import (no MCP), GH Pages workflow with pnpm cache + actions/deploy-pages@v4 (actionlint clean), README link added, 300 workspace specs green, editor build preserved. Pages enablement is a one-time repo-settings step. Commit 606d236.

title: mcp-http-transport-spec — design doc for --http transport
description: mcp-http-transport-spec — bind 127.0.0.1 ONLY (never 0.0.0.0). CORS allowlist defaults to localhost:5173 + localhost:6123, overridable via --cors-origin. No rotating loopback token (same local-only assumption stdio has). Discovery: env var PORTABLE_DOC_MCP_URL with default. Editor-to-localhost path only — playground does not consume MCP. Output: ~/docs/paperflow/specs/2026-05-10-mcp-http-transport.html.
close_reason: T4 verified: spec at ~/docs/paperflow/specs/2026-05-10-mcp-http-transport.html (10 H2 sections, 1 sequence diagram, 4 sources). Locked decisions baked in: bind 127.0.0.1, CORS allowlist localhost:5173+localhost:6123, no rotating token, env-var discovery (PORTABLE_DOC_MCP_URL), editor-only consumer. SDK paths confirmed (StreamableHTTPServerTransport from @modelcontextprotocol/sdk/server/streamableHttp.js).

title: prototype-richtext-callout — sandbox round-trip to InlineNode[]
description: prototype-richtext-callout — Vitest spec rounds-trips InlineNode[] → editor state → InlineNode[] across all 4 text-bearing block contexts (paragraph body, callout body, list-item, action label) without losing bold/em/code/link marks. Sandbox at apps/editor/src/__experiments__/. Disposable; build-phase rebuilds clean.
close_reason: T2 verified: 33 editor specs (27+6), 288 workspace, sandbox isolated under __experiments__/, TipTap v3 deps wired, round-trip clean across paragraph/callout/list-item/action-label contexts with bold/em/code/link marks. Commit ca9492b.

title: research-richtext-engine — decision doc on TipTap vs Lexical vs ProseMirror vs slate
description: research-richtext-engine — TipTap is the prior; doc validates the choice. Comparison table on 7 axes (bundle, React 18 Strict Mode compat HARD requirement, React DX, ecosystem, markdown round-trip, ProseMirror foundation, recent activity). Output: ~/docs/paperflow/notes/2026-05-10-richtext-engine.html. Fall back to Lexical if TipTap fails the Strict Mode gate.
close_reason: T1 verified: decision doc landed at ~/docs/paperflow/notes/2026-05-10-richtext-engine.html. 4 candidates compared (TipTap ✓, Lexical ✓, ProseMirror raw ⚠, slate ⚠). Strict Mode HARD gate passed by TipTap (v3.x, Issue #6988 closed). Final pick: TipTap v3 + @tiptap/starter-kit + @tiptap/extension-bubble-menu. 8 sources cited, 1 mermaid decision tree, 9 H2 sections.
